lines.gurmukhi: ਪੂਰਬ ਜਨਮ ਹਮ ਤੁਮੑਰੇ ਸੇਵਕ ਅਬ ਤਉ ਮਿਟਿਆ ਨ ਜਾਈ ॥
lines.translit: poorab janam ham tumare sevak ab tau mittiaa na jaaee |
lines.english: In my past life, I was Your servant; now, I cannot leave You.

lines.gurmukhi: ਤੇਰੇ ਦੁਆਰੈ ਧੁਨਿ ਸਹਜ ਕੀ ਮਾਥੈ ਮੇਰੇ ਦਗਾਈ ॥੨॥
lines.translit: tere duaarai dhun sehaj kee maathai mere dagaaee |2|
lines.english: The celestial sound current resounds at Your Door. Your insignia is stamped upon my forehead. ||2||

lines.gurmukhi: ਸਾਧੂ ਹੋਇ ਸੁ ਭਗਤਿ ਪਛਾਨੈ ਹਰਿ ਲਏ ਖਜਾਨੈ ਪਾਈ ॥੩॥
lines.translit: saadhoo hoe su bhagat pachhaanai har le khajaanai paaee |3|
lines.english: One who becomes a Holy person, appreciates the value of devotional worship to the Lord. The Lord places him in His treasury. ||3||

lines.gurmukhi: ਦਾਗੇ ਹੋਹਿ ਸੁ ਰਨ ਮਹਿ ਜੂਝਹਿ ਬਿਨੁ ਦਾਗੇ ਭਗਿ ਜਾਈ ॥
lines.translit: daage hohi su ran meh joojheh bin daage bhag jaaee |
lines.english: Those who are branded with Your brand fight bravely in battle; those without Your brand run away.

lines.gurmukhi: ਗੁਰਿ ਦੀਨੀ ਬਸਤੁ ਕਬੀਰ ਕਉ ਲੇਵਹੁ ਬਸਤੁ ਸਮੑਾਰਿ ॥੪॥
lines.translit: gur deenee basat kabeer kau levahu basat samaar |4|
lines.english: The Guru has blessed Kabeer with the commodity, saying, "Take this commodity; cherish it and keep it secure." ||4||

lines.gurmukhi: ਕੋਠਰੇ ਮਹਿ ਕੋਠਰੀ ਪਰਮ ਕੋਠੀ ਬੀਚਾਰਿ ॥
lines.translit: kotthare meh kottharee param kotthee beechaar |
lines.english: In the fortress is the chamber; by contemplative meditation it becomes the supreme chamber.

lines.gurmukhi: ਕਬੀਰਿ ਦੀਈ ਸੰਸਾਰ ਕਉ ਲੀਨੀ ਜਿਸੁ ਮਸਤਕਿ ਭਾਗੁ ॥
lines.translit: kabeer deeee sansaar kau leenee jis masatak bhaag |
lines.english: Kabeer gives it to the world, but he alone receives it, upon whose forehead such destiny is recorded.

lines.gurmukhi: ਜਿਹ ਮੁਖ ਬੇਦੁ ਗਾਇਤ੍ਰੀ ਨਿਕਸੈ ਸੋ ਕਿਉ ਬ੍ਰਹਮਨੁ ਬਿਸਰੁ ਕਰੈ ॥
lines.translit: jih mukh bed gaaeitree nikasai so kiau brahaman bisar karai |
lines.english: O Brahmin, how can you forget the One, from whose mouth the Vedas and the Gayitri prayer issured forth?

lines.gurmukhi: ਅੰਮ੍ਰਿਤ ਰਸੁ ਜਿਨਿ ਪਾਇਆ ਥਿਰੁ ਤਾ ਕਾ ਸੋਹਾਗੁ ॥੫॥੪॥
lines.translit: amrit ras jin paaeaa thir taa kaa sohaag |5|4|
lines.english: Permanent is the marriage, of one who receives this ambrosial essence. ||5||4||

lines.gurmukhi: ਕਾਹੇ ਮੇਰੇ ਬਾਮੑਨ ਹਰਿ ਨ ਕਹਹਿ ॥
lines.translit: kaahe mere baaman har na kaheh |
lines.english: Why, O my Brahmin, do you not chant the Lord's Name?

lines.gurmukhi: ਜਾ ਕੈ ਪਾਇ ਜਗਤੁ ਸਭੁ ਲਾਗੈ ਸੋ ਕਿਉ ਪੰਡਿਤੁ ਹਰਿ ਨ ਕਹੈ ॥੧॥
lines.translit: jaa kai paae jagat sabh laagai so kiau panddit har na kahai |1|
lines.english: The whole world falls at His feet; why don't you chant the Name of that Lord, O Pandit? ||1||

lines.gurmukhi: ਰਾਮੁ ਨ ਬੋਲਹਿ ਪਾਡੇ ਦੋਜਕੁ ਭਰਹਿ ॥੧॥ ਰਹਾਉ ॥
lines.translit: raam na boleh paadde dojak bhareh |1| rahaau |
lines.english: If you don't chant the Lord's Name, O Pandit, you will only suffer in hell. ||1||Pause||

lines.gurmukhi: ਆਪਨ ਊਚ ਨੀਚ ਘਰਿ ਭੋਜਨੁ ਹਠੇ ਕਰਮ ਕਰਿ ਉਦਰੁ ਭਰਹਿ ॥
lines.translit: aapan aooch neech ghar bhojan hatthe karam kar udar bhareh |
lines.english: You think that you are high, but you take food from the houses of the lowly; you fill up your belly by forcibly practicing your rituals.

lines.gurmukhi: ਤੂੰ ਬ੍ਰਹਮਨੁ ਮੈ ਕਾਸੀਕ ਜੁਲਹਾ ਮੁਹਿ ਤੋਹਿ ਬਰਾਬਰੀ ਕੈਸੇ ਕੈ ਬਨਹਿ ॥
lines.translit: toon brahaman mai kaaseek julahaa muhi tohi baraabaree kaise kai baneh |
lines.english: You are a Brahmin, and I am only a weaver from Benares. How can I compare to you?

lines.gurmukhi: ਚਉਦਸ ਅਮਾਵਸ ਰਚਿ ਰਚਿ ਮਾਂਗਹਿ ਕਰ ਦੀਪਕੁ ਲੈ ਕੂਪਿ ਪਰਹਿ ॥੨॥
lines.translit: chaudas amaavas rach rach maangeh kar deepak lai koop pareh |2|
lines.english: On the fourteenth day, and the night of the new moon, you go out begging; even though you hold the lamp in your hands, still, you fall into the pit. ||2||

lines.gurmukhi: ਹਮਰੇ ਰਾਮ ਨਾਮ ਕਹਿ ਉਬਰੇ ਬੇਦ ਭਰੋਸੇ ਪਾਂਡੇ ਡੂਬਿ ਮਰਹਿ ॥੩॥੫॥
lines.translit: hamare raam naam keh ubare bed bharose paandde ddoob mareh |3|5|
lines.english: Chanting the Lord's Name, I have been saved; relying on the Vedas, O Brahmin, you shall drown and die. ||3||5||

lines.gurmukhi: ਇਹ ਅੰਮ੍ਰਿਤ ਕੀ ਬਾੜੀ ਹੈ ਰੇ ਤਿਨਿ ਹਰਿ ਪੂਰੈ ਕਰੀਆ ॥੧॥
lines.translit: eih amrit kee baarree hai re tin har poorai kareea |1|
lines.english: This world is a garden of Ambrosial Nectar. The Perfect Lord created it. ||1||

lines.gurmukhi: ਤਰਵਰੁ ਏਕੁ ਅਨੰਤ ਡਾਰ ਸਾਖਾ ਪੁਹਪ ਪਤ੍ਰ ਰਸ ਭਰੀਆ ॥
lines.translit: taravar ek anant ddaar saakhaa puhap patr ras bhareea |
lines.english: There is a single tree, with countless branches and twigs; its flowers and leaves are filled with its juice.

lines.gurmukhi: ਜਾਨੀ ਜਾਨੀ ਰੇ ਰਾਜਾ ਰਾਮ ਕੀ ਕਹਾਨੀ ॥
lines.translit: jaanee jaanee re raajaa raam kee kahaanee |
lines.english: I have come to know the story of my Sovereign Lord.

lines.gurmukhi: ਅੰਤਰਿ ਜੋਤਿ ਰਾਮ ਪਰਗਾਸਾ ਗੁਰਮੁਖਿ ਬਿਰਲੈ ਜਾਨੀ ॥੧॥ ਰਹਾਉ ॥
lines.translit: antar jot raam paragaasaa guramukh biralai jaanee |1| rahaau |
lines.english: How rare is that Gurmukh who knows, and whose inner being is illumined by the Lord's Light. ||1||Pause||

lines.gurmukhi: ਸੋਰਹ ਮਧੇ ਪਵਨੁ ਝਕੋਰਿਆ ਆਕਾਸੇ ਫਰੁ ਫਰਿਆ ॥੨॥
lines.translit: sorah madhe pavan jhakoriaa aakaase far fariaa |2|
lines.english: He holds his breath suspended in the sixteen-petalled sky of the Akaashic Ethers, and beats his wings in esctasy. ||2||

lines.gurmukhi: ਭਵਰੁ ਏਕੁ ਪੁਹਪ ਰਸ ਬੀਧਾ ਬਾਰਹ ਲੇ ਉਰ ਧਰਿਆ ॥
lines.translit: bhavar ek puhap ras beedhaa baarah le ur dhariaa |
lines.english: The bumble bee, addicted to the nectar of the twelve-petalled flowers, enshrines it in the heart.

lines.gurmukhi: ਸਹਜ ਸੁੰਨਿ ਇਕੁ ਬਿਰਵਾ ਉਪਜਿਆ ਧਰਤੀ ਜਲਹਰੁ ਸੋਖਿਆ ॥
lines.translit: sehaj sun ik biravaa upajiaa dharatee jalahar sokhiaa |
lines.english: In the profound void of intuitive Samaadhi, the one tree rises up; it soaks up the water of desire from the ground.

lines.gurmukhi: ਕਹਿ ਕਬੀਰ ਹਉ ਤਾ ਕਾ ਸੇਵਕੁ ਜਿਨਿ ਇਹੁ ਬਿਰਵਾ ਦੇਖਿਆ ॥੩॥੬॥
lines.translit: keh kabeer hau taa kaa sevak jin ihu biravaa dekhiaa |3|6|
lines.english: Says Kabeer, I am the servant of those who have seen this celestial tree. ||3||6||

lines.gurmukhi: ਮੁੰਦ੍ਰਾ ਮੋਨਿ ਦਇਆ ਕਰਿ ਝੋਲੀ ਪਤ੍ਰ ਕਾ ਕਰਹੁ ਬੀਚਾਰੁ ਰੇ ॥
lines.translit: mundraa mon deaa kar jholee patr kaa karahu beechaar re |
lines.english: Make silence your ear-rings, and compassion your wallet; let meditation be your begging bowl.

lines.gurmukhi: ਖਿੰਥਾ ਇਹੁ ਤਨੁ ਸੀਅਉ ਅਪਨਾ ਨਾਮੁ ਕਰਉ ਆਧਾਰੁ ਰੇ ॥੧॥
lines.translit: khinthaa ihu tan seeo apanaa naam krau aadhaar re |1|
lines.english: Sew this body as your patched coat, and take the Lord's Name as your support. ||1||

lines.gurmukhi: ਐਸਾ ਜੋਗੁ ਕਮਾਵਹੁ ਜੋਗੀ ॥
lines.translit: aisaa jog kamaavahu jogee |
lines.english: Practice such Yoga, O Yogi.

lines.gurmukhi: ਬੁਧਿ ਬਿਭੂਤਿ ਚਢਾਵਉ ਅਪੁਨੀ ਸਿੰਗੀ ਸੁਰਤਿ ਮਿਲਾਈ ॥
lines.translit: budh bibhoot chadtaavau apunee singee surat milaaee |
lines.english: Apply the ashes of wisdom to your body; let your horn be your focused consciousness.

lines.gurmukhi: ਜਪ ਤਪ ਸੰਜਮੁ ਗੁਰਮੁਖਿ ਭੋਗੀ ॥੧॥ ਰਹਾਉ ॥
lines.translit: jap tap sanjam guramukh bhogee |1| rahaau |
lines.english: As Gurmukh, enjoy meditation, austerities and self-discipline. ||1||Pause||

lines.gurmukhi: ਕਰਿ ਬੈਰਾਗੁ ਫਿਰਉ ਤਨਿ ਨਗਰੀ ਮਨ ਕੀ ਕਿੰਗੁਰੀ ਬਜਾਈ ॥੨॥
lines.translit: kar bairaag firau tan nagaree man kee kinguree bajaaee |2|
lines.english: Become detached, and wander through the city of your body; play the harp of your mind. ||2||

lines.gurmukhi: ਪੰਚ ਤਤੁ ਲੈ ਹਿਰਦੈ ਰਾਖਹੁ ਰਹੈ ਨਿਰਾਲਮ ਤਾੜੀ ॥
lines.translit: panch tat lai hiradai raakhahu rahai niraalam taarree |
lines.english: Enshrine the five tatvas - the five elements, within your heart; let your deep meditative trance be undisturbed.

lines.gurmukhi: ਕਹਤੁ ਕਬੀਰੁ ਸੁਨਹੁ ਰੇ ਸੰਤਹੁ ਧਰਮੁ ਦਇਆ ਕਰਿ ਬਾੜੀ ॥੩॥੭॥
lines.translit: kehat kabeer sunahu re santahu dharam deaa kar baarree |3|7|
lines.english: Says Kabeer, listen, O Saints: make righteousness and compassion your garden. ||3||7||

lines.gurmukhi: ਭਵ ਨਿਧਿ ਤਰਨ ਤਾਰਨ ਚਿੰਤਾਮਨਿ ਇਕ ਨਿਮਖ ਨ ਇਹੁ ਮਨੁ ਲਾਇਆ ॥੧॥
lines.translit: bhav nidh taran taaran chintaaman ik nimakh na ihu man laaeaa |1|
lines.english: God is the boat to carry you across the terrifying world-ocean; He is the Fulfiller of the mind's desires. You have not centered your mind on Him, even for an instant. ||1||

lines.gurmukhi: ਕਵਨ ਕਾਜ ਸਿਰਜੇ ਜਗ ਭੀਤਰਿ ਜਨਮਿ ਕਵਨ ਫਲੁ ਪਾਇਆ ॥
lines.translit: kavan kaaj siraje jag bheetar janam kavan fal paaeaa |
lines.english: For what purpose were you created and brought into the world? What rewards have you received in this life?
